phases.name: Pin the SQL every existing view and report compiles to today so no later slice changes rendered output by accident

steps.name: Pin compiled SQL for every seeded view and report
steps.criteria: The characterization suite emits and pins the compiled DuckDB SQL for every seeded view and report; the golden snapshot is captured from real seeded/dev relations.
steps.scenario_name: Every seeded view and report pins its compiled SQL

steps.name: Fail the snapshot on a deliberate SQL change
steps.criteria: A deliberate change to any relation's rendered SQL makes the suite fail and reports a per-relation diff identifying which relation drifted.
steps.scenario_name: A deliberate change to rendered SQL fails the snapshot with a per-relation diff

steps.name: Prove re-rendering with no change is deterministic
steps.criteria: Re-rendering the same relations with no change produces byte-identical SQL (stable ordering, no timestamps) — the snapshot is deterministic across runs.
steps.scenario_name: Re-rendering with no change is deterministic

phases.name: Lift Report's columns_metadata dict-soup onto Pydantic discriminated unions so a malformed column is rejected at the boundary, never at render time

steps.name: Reject a column with an unknown semantic role
steps.criteria: A report column carrying an unknown semantic_role is rejected at the boundary with a structured 422/Failure and nothing is persisted.
steps.scenario_name: A report column with an unknown semantic role is rejected at the boundary

steps.name: Reject an illegal role/type pairing via the typed union
steps.criteria: A report column with an illegal role-and-type pairing is rejected by the discriminated union itself, not by a free validation function.
steps.scenario_name: A report column with an illegal role and type pairing is rejected by the typed union

steps.name: Load every existing report through the typed projection kernel
steps.criteria: Every existing report hydrates and validates through the new typed projection kernel (production shape coverage).
steps.scenario_name: Every existing report loads through the typed projection kernel

phases.name: Collapse the View/Report compilers' shared steps into one kernel visitor the report extension composes, with a build-time completeness check

steps.name: Produce byte-identical SQL from the consolidated renderer
steps.criteria: The consolidated kernel renderer produces SQL byte-identical to the characterization snapshot for every seeded relation.
steps.scenario_name: The consolidated renderer produces byte-identical SQL to the characterization snapshot

steps.name: Fail the build on an unhandled component discriminator
steps.criteria: An unhandled component discriminator fails the build/test at completeness-check time rather than being silently skipped at render time.
steps.scenario_name: An unhandled component discriminator fails the build instead of silently skipping

steps.name: Render an entity-only report through the shared kernel path
steps.criteria: An entity-only report renders through the shared kernel path as kernel output with no aggregation step — not via a special-case branch.
steps.scenario_name: An entity-only report renders through the shared kernel path with no aggregation step

phases.name: Disaggregate the filters JSON array into the first normalized component table, establishing the shared component-table repository and polymorphic cascade pattern

steps.name: Write exactly one filter row when adding a filter
steps.criteria: Adding one filter results in a single-row INSERT into relation_filters with no rewrite of any JSON array.
steps.scenario_name: Adding one filter writes exactly one filter row

steps.name: Backfill every embedded filter into one keyed row
steps.criteria: The expand/contract backfill explodes each embedded JSON filter into exactly one keyed relation_filters row, and the renderer's filter step reads from rows.
steps.scenario_name: The backfill turns every embedded filter into one keyed row

steps.name: Keep rendered SQL unchanged when filters are reordered
steps.criteria: Reordering two filters (which are commutative, no order column) leaves the rendered SQL unchanged.
steps.scenario_name: Reordering two filters leaves the rendered SQL unchanged

steps.name: Cascade-delete only the parent's own filter rows
steps.criteria: Deleting a relation removes exactly its own filter rows and no other relation's rows, via the repo-enforced polymorphic cascade.
steps.scenario_name: Deleting a relation removes only its own filter rows

steps.name: Scope filter loads to the relation's tenant
steps.criteria: Loading a relation's filters is scoped to its org_id/tenant, backed by the composite (org_id, parent_type, parent_id) index.
steps.scenario_name: Loading a relation's filters is scoped to its tenant

phases.name: Move both view and report projected columns into one shared relation_columns table, proving the shared-kernel projection across roles

steps.name: List views and reports projecting a shared column
steps.criteria: One cross-role query (WHERE output_name='X') returns both the views and the reports that project the same column from relation_columns.
steps.scenario_name: One query lists every view and report projecting the same column

steps.name: Keep rendered SQL unchanged when columns are reordered
steps.criteria: Reordering columns leaves the rendered SQL byte-identical to the characterization snapshot.
steps.scenario_name: Reordering columns leaves the rendered SQL unchanged

steps.name: Keep rendered SQL unchanged when presentation position changes
steps.criteria: Changing a column's presentation-only position leaves the rendered SQL unchanged.
steps.scenario_name: Changing a column's presentation position leaves the rendered SQL unchanged

steps.name: Write exactly one column row when adding a column
steps.criteria: Adding one column results in a single-row INSERT into relation_columns for either role.
steps.scenario_name: Adding one column writes exactly one column row

phases.name: Normalize joins with an explicit declaration-ordered sequence, preserving compiled SQL and making reorder deterministic

steps.name: Produce different SQL when two joins' sequence is swapped
steps.criteria: Swapping the sequence of two relation_joins rows produces different rendered SQL, proving join order is correctness-bearing.
steps.scenario_name: Swapping two joins' sequence produces different SQL

steps.name: Keep rendered SQL byte-identical when joins read in declaration order
steps.criteria: Joins read from rows in sequence (declaration) order leave the rendered SQL byte-identical to the characterization snapshot.
steps.scenario_name: Joins read in declaration order keep the rendered SQL byte-identical

steps.name: Assign join sequence from array position in the backfill
steps.criteria: The backfill assigns relation_joins.sequence from the JSON array position, not from created_at.
steps.scenario_name: The backfill assigns join sequence from array position not creation time

phases.name: Store a relation's grain (time column plus dimension keys) as queryable rows, feeding the report aggregation step from one place

steps.name: Query a relation's grain keys as rows
steps.criteria: A relation's grain keys are readable as relation_grain rows, feeding the renderer/report extension from one place.
steps.scenario_name: A relation's grain keys are queryable as rows

steps.name: Keep rendered SQL unchanged when grain keys are reordered
steps.criteria: Reordering grain keys (set-like, no order column) leaves the rendered SQL unchanged.
steps.scenario_name: Reordering grain keys leaves the rendered SQL unchanged

steps.name: Produce exactly one grain row per relation in the backfill
steps.criteria: The backfill from views.grain JSON produces exactly one grain row per relation (OQ-3 one-row-per-parent cardinality).
steps.scenario_name: The backfill produces exactly one grain row per relation

phases.name: Add the report-only aggregation table and enforce measure-requires-dimension and no-mart-to-mart over typed rows

steps.name: Reject a measure with no dimension over typed rows
steps.criteria: A report binding a measure with no dimension is rejected with ReportRequiresDimension evaluated over typed rows, not dict probes.
steps.scenario_name: A report with a measure and no dimension is rejected over typed rows

steps.name: Reject a report sourcing another report via the composition service
steps.criteria: A report sourcing another report is rejected with InvalidReportReference by the shared composition service, peer to View's circular-dependency check.
steps.scenario_name: A report sourcing another report is rejected by the composition service

steps.name: Write exactly one aggregation row when binding a valid measure
steps.criteria: Binding a valid measure results in a single-row INSERT into relation_aggregations.
steps.scenario_name: Binding a valid measure writes exactly one aggregation row

steps.name: Keep rendered SQL unchanged when aggregations are reordered
steps.criteria: Reordering aggregations (independent, no order column) leaves the rendered SQL unchanged.
steps.scenario_name: Reordering aggregations leaves the rendered SQL unchanged

phases.name: Remove the now-unread embedded-JSON columns after one safe release, leaving one source of truth

steps.name: Keep rendered SQL byte-identical after dropping the embedded JSON columns
steps.criteria: Dropping the embedded component columns (views.{columns,joins,filters,grain}, reports.columns_metadata) leaves the rendered SQL byte-identical to the characterization snapshot.
steps.scenario_name: Dropping the embedded component columns keeps the rendered SQL byte-identical

steps.name: Exercise the contract migration's rollback path
steps.criteria: The contract migration has a rollback path that re-adds the dropped columns and re-backfills them from rows, exercised in a test.
steps.scenario_name: The contract migration has a rollback path that re-adds and re-backfills the columns
